raw_response:
I agree with the conclusion provided by Agents 1 and 2. While there might initially seem conflicting views, both points emphasize the importance of applying forces judiciously to promote healing while avoiding harm. Specifically, my agreement lies in acknowledging that although posterior loading typically creates tension on the upper border due to muscle action (as noted), its application also often aims to provide necessary support and compression on the lower part of the mandible to maintain structural integrity. Therefore, considering all evidence presented, Option B ("Compression on Medial Surface") seems more aligned with ensuring overall stability and preventing displacement of the fracture segments. 

Answer Choice: B.
